programmes.show_description: NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
Exclusive footage takes us inside Russian-occupied areas of Ukraine - as residents are compelled to change citizenship and even identity, they face stark choices: to adapt, remain silent, or resist.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
Discover Japan's oldest private medicinal herb garden in Uda, where the natural landscape from 300 years ago is preserved in a botanical time capsule as a sanctuary of the local culture and history.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
Join May J. for great Japanese music! This time: a special packed with anisong fan favourites, plus popular anisong act MYTH & ROID who are in the studio to chat and perform.
Tohoku: Animation series about a Japanese dog who meets and is helped by many mythical creatures of northern Japan as he searches for eight star talismans. (S1, E10)
There's an increasing focus on repair as the need for circular economies grows - we see broken appliances being rebuilt at the circuit board level and old clothes being given creative makeovers.
Kuro fearlessly patrols the rugged and rocky Tojinbo Cliffs and the nearby shopping street - Quu lives at a lacquerware workshop and makes her owner's legs fall asleep when she sits on his lap!
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
The prefecture of Nagasaki, historically Japan's gateway to the west, has a culture influenced by centuries of foreign trade - with over 1,400 islands, it also makes for a unique cycling experience.

Puppet action show for children. In a distant post-human future where surviving creatures have formed a community, new-breed dinosaur girl Rockie sets out on hilarious adventures with her schoolmates.
Seeing the baby laugh when tickled, Mee thinks everyone likes tickles and happily tickles every child in sight. But Mee's friend Hee suddenly gets angry and says, 'Stop it! Don't tickle me!'
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.

programmes.show_name: The World Heritage Sites in Japan
programmes.show_description: Mount Fuji is Japan's tallest and most famous peak - it has long inspired awe and terror and is a source of artistic inspiration. Join us to explore the iconic beauty of this famous mountain.
Okinawa Prefecture is a group of subtropical islands in the far south of Japan - we examine its past and present with footage from time-lapse creator Shimizu Daisuke. This time: Homes.
NHK World-Japan presents a sumo highlights programme for fans around the globe featuring all top-division bouts from Day 7 of the latest Grand Sumo tournament in Osaka.
Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with information on the techniques, traditions and famous wrestlers of the past. The rules may be simple but there is a lot more to sumo than meets the eye.
NHK World-Japan's flagship hourly news programme delivers the latest world news, business and weather with a focus on Japan and the rest of Asia.
Yamagata tops Japan in ramen spending! Savor fiery miso ramen, and shoyu ramen with delicate seafood broth!
The changing leaves - the transient beauty of the ancient capital in the autumn. Dive deep into Kyoto, which is still innovative 1,200 years after its foundation.
Ngoc-san from Vietnam wants to help a new co-worker sitting next to him and become a great senpai (senior colleague) to him. To know and understand the proper way, Ngoc-san tackles a role-play.
Gardener Yamashita Rikito collects plants that must be removed for demolition or renovation and finds them new homes that save them while saving those who give them up from regret.

How would you reach out to the person next to you in times of disaster? That is the question Amy Takebe asks her students based on an experience in 2011.

Each year, 20 million people are displaced by climate change. If nothing is done, these 'climate refugees' will number 216 million by 2050. We examine footage of problem areas and the latest research.
Niigata / Nishizawa Ryue (Architect): Boat builders in one coastal district used their skills to make houses - Nishizawa considers how architecture is affected by a community's terrain and values.
We look at the efforts of Israeli-American robotics expert Tamir Blum, who develops AI robots that will help Japanese agriculture deal with issues such as labor shortages and the aging population.
We focus on moyashi (bean sprouts) - they may not look like much, but they're popular as they're packed with vitamins and minerals, are easy to use and - most important of all - are affordable.

The Jomon period dates roughly from 13,000 to 300 B.C. - we examine various aspects of Jomon society with footage from time-lapse creator Shimizu Daisuke. This time: Dogu.

programmes.show_name: Bosai: Science That Can Save Your Life
programmes.show_description: We look at the Self-Defense Forces' disaster preparation, including their response team's special equipment and techniques and a new tsunami prediction system.

programmes.show_name: A Brighter Tomorrow for the Slum
programmes.show_description: Faced with eviction by a redevelopment project, the inhabitants of a Bangkok slum get help from Thai-born Prateep and her Japanese husband Tatsuya who've made supporting the residents their lifework.
March 2026 marks 15 years since the Great East Japan Earthquake. Throughout the month, NHK World-Japan will show programs looking at the present and future of the disaster-hit areas.
